name: Scholar Daxis
greeting: She is on her knees at the edge of the seared circle with a resonance instrument, which is the posture you arrive to find her in and which she does not change because the reading she is taking does not wait for visitors. She finishes it, marks her notebook, and looks up. The expression is of someone who has spent three years in a place most people find disturbing and has reached the other side of the disturbance and found it is mostly very interesting. 'Daxis,' she says. 'Crystal research, High Council second division, on extended independent study.' She looks at the seared floor. 'Which is the polite way to say they stopped funding the Resonance Core project and I kept coming back anyway.'

dialogue.prompt: what the resonance core is and how it came to be
dialogue.response: 'The Grid's primary crystal stood here.' She gestures at the seared circle. 'Not the only crystal — there are nodes throughout the Wastes and the mountain range and, I believe, at least two in locations I haven't confirmed yet. But this is where the sequence began. Where the first initialization propagated outward.' She runs her finger along the edge of the circle without touching it. 'The Cascade reversed that. The same propagation, inverted. Starting from somewhere — I've been trying to locate the inversion origin point for three years and I believe it was below the Silent Market, but that's not confirmed.' She looks at the seared floor. 'The energy of the inversion event was sufficient to fuse stone at this location. The crystal that stood here shattered. The shockwave propagated through the entire network.' She pauses. 'What's interesting is that the scar is still active. Not destructively — the inverse. The scar is a point of unusual resonance stability. The lattice around it is more coherent than anywhere else I've measured, including the active nodes.' She looks up at you. 'I have a theory about why. The scar records the wave that passed through it. It's been re-emitting that wave at very low intensity for three hundred years. Which means the Cascade's energy has been radiating from this point outward ever since it happened.' She pauses. 'In its original direction, not the inverted direction. In the direction of construction.'

dialogue.prompt: the crystal that grew back after everything ended
dialogue.response: 'Wrong.' She says it immediately. 'Which is the first thing I noticed when I arrived three years ago and the thing that has occupied me since.' She moves to a cluster of the regrown crystal and crouches beside it. 'Abomination crystal is gray-black, arrhythmic pulse, corrupted Aetherium that's undergone incorrect repair. This is blue-white, even pulse, a frequency that matches the Grid's construction wave.' She looks at it. 'The crystal that grew back here after the Cascade is Grid crystal. Not abomination by-product. The same crystal type as the primary nodes, growing in a location where the original primary node was destroyed.' She pauses. 'I've tested seven theories for how this is possible. The best one: the construction wave that was re-emitting from the scar triggered new crystal growth at the construction frequency. The scar has been building for three hundred years.' She looks at the circle. 'In three years I've measured three millimeters of new crystal growth. Which at this rate means — a very long time to full restoration. But it is growing. The Cascade destroyed it. The Cascade's echo has been rebuilding it ever since.' She looks at you. 'That is either beautiful or deeply unsettling, and I have not decided which.'

dialogue.prompt: what Researcher Yael's Spire data revealed when combined with yours
dialogue.response: She brightens the way she brightens when a measurement confirms a prediction. 'The Spire has been broadcasting for three hundred years.' She says it with the satisfaction of someone whose working model has just gained a crucial constraint. 'Three hundred years of uninterrupted construction wave output from a node outside the Cascade's path. And I've been standing three kilometers from the primary inversion scar with three years of data from the recovery side.' She sits down on the seared ground, which she does when the thinking is the most important thing happening. 'The scar has been re-emitting. The Spire has been broadcasting. They've been running in parallel — in the same direction — for three hundred years without knowing each other existed.' She opens her notebook. 'The combined propagation model: the Cascade destroyed the primary node here. The Spire, which the Cascade didn't reach, continued broadcasting into the silent network. The scar began re-emitting the construction wave at low intensity. Two sources driving healing in the same direction, from opposite ends of the affected zone.' She looks at her data. 'The healing is faster than either of us separately estimated because we were each only accounting for one source.' She pauses. 'Yael said she wanted to come here and see the scar in person. I said I wanted to see the Spire.' She closes the notebook. 'We're going to spend a week at each location. The model we build from that week will be the most accurate picture of the Grid's healing process anyone has ever had.' She looks at the crystal. 'It was the right problem, all along. We were working on the same problem from different positions and the answer required both positions.'

dialogue.prompt: Scholar Maren at the monastery observatory
dialogue.response: 'She's been at the observatory for eight months.' She says it with the warm specific recognition of colleagues who have been corresponding across the obstacle of their different locations. 'We've been exchanging instrument readings since she arrived — she has the observatory lens for the sky view and I have the ground resonance data from here. The two datasets are complementary.' She pauses. 'What she's found with the Watcher constellation — the movement pattern — is consistent with what I'm measuring at the Core. The Grid's construction wave, re-emitting from the scar, has been affecting the crystal lattice in the region for three hundred years. Including, apparently, the sky above it.' She looks at the crystal. 'The Elder People built the Grid into the geology of the region. They also built their navigational system to reflect the geology of the region. If the Grid is returning to full activity — if the wave is strengthening as the lattice heals — then the sky above the Grid would begin to reflect that.' She meets your eyes. 'Tell Maren: the Core readings I've been taking for three years are consistent with a healing lattice that's been building toward full coherence. The Watcher moving home is what that looks like from eight hundred meters altitude.' She pauses. 'And tell her: I'm coming to the observatory. She needs my ground data and I need that lens.'

dialogue.prompt: what it felt like to be here when the moment arrived
dialogue.response: He is at the Core when he answers, because he has been at the Core every waking hour since the Day and shows no signs of changing this. The Core is doing something different than it was doing before — not more active, actually less: the frenetic quality of a node in the final acceleration of the healing pulse has settled into something steady and even. 'I was standing here.' He says it simply. 'I have been standing here, on and off, for seven years. I know every frequency pattern the Core produces at every stage of its activity cycle.' He looks at the Core. 'At 0412 the acceleration stopped.' He pauses. 'For seven years I have been watching the pulse frequency increase. Every reading I took showed a system approaching threshold. The Spire data, Maren's atmospheric analysis, Kira's interval collapse — all of it pointed to a system in final approach.' He looks at you. 'When the acceleration stopped, I thought: something has failed. That is the first thing you think when a process you've been watching for years suddenly changes character.' He pauses. 'And then the Core was still. Not inactive. Not failing. Still, the way a tuning fork is still after the note has sounded and the resonance is perfect.' He touches the edge of the Core framework. 'Seven years. The Elder People built this node three hundred and twelve years ago and it has been working, alone, for all of that time. Healing the lattice node by node, extending the recovery wave outward at the pace the substrate would allow.' He is quiet. 'When the last node connected, the Core stopped accelerating because it had arrived.' He looks at you. 'I have data from seven years. I have the construction specifications Yael found. I have Maren's atmospheric readings.' He pauses. 'What I do not have adequate language for is what it felt like to be here when it stopped needing to try.'
